title: Hard-remove legacy config + migration backward-compat (rules.toml sole source)
description:
Hard-remove the legacy config + migration backward-compat machinery so `rules.toml` is the sole validation source. Only jit + gf2 exist pre-1.0 and both are migrated (Task C), so no legacy support is needed.

## Background

Part of Scope Expansion 2. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-expansion2-plan.md` §3 and §6a (MF1/MF2/MF3/MF4). Depends on Task C (migrate gf2 before deleting the migration code).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `crates/jit/src/validation/migration.rs` (config→rules migration, coexistence, materialize-existing, deprecated-key scan) is removed; only a fixed-default scaffold path remains for `jit init`.
- [ ] [hard] Legacy `[validation]` enforcement keys and `NamespaceConfig.{values,pattern,required}` parsing are removed (incl. `config_manager.rs` and all struct literals); `default_type`/`strictness` and the namespace registry + type_hierarchy are retained.
- [ ] [hard] `default_ruleset(namespaces)` is the fixed default emitting exactly: label-format (e=true), namespace-registry (e=false, when registry non-empty), type-hierarchy-known (e=false), namespace-unique:<ns> (e=true), and UNCONDITIONAL orphan-leaf + strategic-consistency (warn).
- [ ] [hard] An absent `rules.toml` builds defaults IN MEMORY on the read path (no write-on-read); only `jit init` materializes it to disk under the write lock.
- [ ] [hard] Fresh-init uses the lean default (no namespace value/pattern/required starter rules); `intended_default_config` + tests updated; DR §8.2/§8.4 and docs/reference config docs updated.
- [ ] [hard] Acceptance: `jit init` in this repo yields an empty `git diff` on `.jit/rules.toml`; `cargo fmt`, `clippy --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo test --workspace` clean.
